text: So far, there is no unified definition of seawater intrusion. Xue Yuqun believes: 'If the amount of fresh water pumped exceeds its replenishment, causing the groundwater head near the coastal zone to drop to a level where the head of the freshwater body is lower than that of the nearby seawater wedge-shaped body, the interface between salt and fresh water will advance towards the land until a new equilibrium is formed. This is seawater intrusion.' %3 '; Jiang Jiali believes that: 'Seawater intrusion refers to the phenomenon in coastal areas where, due to excessive exploitation of groundwater, the depth of groundwater continues to decline, the dynamic balance between salt and fresh water is disrupted, and seawater directly invades the freshwater aquifer.'' Wang Bingchen believes: 'Seawater intrusion is a natural phenomenon in coastal areas where seawater directly invades the freshwater layer due to the decline in the groundwater level of the land.'' $'.'' Other scholars have also published similar definitions. From the definitions provided in many documents, although the literal expressions may not be consistent, it can be abstracted that the essence of seawater intrusion is 'excessive exploitation of groundwater by humans in coastal areas, causing a significant drop in the groundwater level, disrupting the dynamic balance between seawater and freshwater, and leading to the movement of the brackish and freshwater boundary towards the land.' Therefore, the author defines seawater intrusion as: 'In coastal areas, excessive exploitation of groundwater by humans causes a significant drop in the groundwater level, disrupts the hydrodynamic balance between seawater and fresh water, and leads to the displacement of the brackish and fresh water interface towards the land direction.' The core aspect of the definition of seawater intrusion is 'the disruption of hydrodynamic balance caused by excessive exploitation of groundwater by human activities'. In other words, seawater intrusion is caused by human factors, while natural factors only play a certain role in influencing and controlling it.

At present,  is being affected by seawater intrusion in many coastal areas around the world. At least 11 cities and regions in , China, have experienced varying degrees of seawater intrusion. Seawater intrusion in coastal areas often leads to environmental problems such as deteriorating water quality and land salinization , which greatly restricts the economic development of coastal areas. To treat the degraded environment  would cost more. Usually, when  is used to study the problem of seawater intrusion, two models are employed: the abrupt interface model and the transition zone model. Early  many scholars applied the abrupt interface model to two-dimensional planar and sectional simulation studies of seawater intrusion. In fact,  fresh water and seawater are miscible , and there exists a brackish transition zone between them . In most cases, the width of the transition zone cannot be ignored . At this time, the transition zone model should be used. Henry [1] based on the immiscible body , published for the first time a two-dimensional analytical solution of the salt concentration profile on a vertical section orthogonal to the coastline , which became the benchmark for numerical experiments of the transition zone model. Pinder and Voss et al. successively established various two-dimensional finite element models of profiles  and applied them to the calculation of seawater intrusion in many regions. So far  most seawater intrusion models are limited to single-layer two-dimensional models. Huyakorn [2] proposed the density-dependent groundwater flow equation and migration equation  and established a three-dimensional finite element model of the seawater intrusion transition zone in the coastal multi-layer aquifer. Xue Yuqun [3] established a three-dimensional characteristic finite element model  to study seawater intrusion in a coastal aquifer in longkou, shandong. Wu Jichun [4] established a mathematical model of seawater intrusion reflecting the cation exchange between water and rock.

In the early years, seawater intrusion occurred along the coast of Laizhou Bay in Shandong Province. Later, saltwater intrusion also occurred. The seawater intrusion mainly took place from Zhenzhu Township in Laizhou City to the right bank of Huangshui River in Longkou City. The saltwater intrusion mainly occurs in the section from Tushan Township in Laizhou City through Changyi and Shouguang to the south bank of the Xiaoqing River in Guangrao County. These two phenomena are often collectively referred to as saltwater intrusion abroad and have long been the focus of academic attention The fruit of research is achieved through benevolence. Chuan et al. commented that many papers noted that ion exchange would occur during the invasion process, but there was a lack of good field data to confirm that domestic research began in the late 2000s. There have been few comprehensive and systematic studies since the beginning of the year. A systematic study was conducted on these two phenomena that have brought great harm to the local area. The study area includes the coastal districts of Longkou, Zhaoyuan and Laizhou cities, Hanting District of Weifang City and Changyi County

(1) The seawater intrusion at the Yangtze River Estuary and the Pearl River Estuary is mainly influenced by the size of the runoff from the rivers flowing into the sea and the tides at the estuaries. In the Laizhou Bay area, where there is a severe problem of seawater intrusion, the occurrence of seawater intrusion is mainly due to excessive exploitation of underground fresh water by humans. (2) There exists a critical plane of dynamic equilibrium between freshwater and seawater on land in coastal areas. Once a large amount of groundwater is exploited or the sea level rises, it will lead to the disruption of the equilibrium state. The critical plane will then move towards the land, and the storage space for fresh water in the aquifer will be replaced by seawater. As a result, seawater intrusion will occur. (3) Besides hydrogeological conditions and topographic and geomorphic conditions, climatic conditions and human activities are also important factors contributing to seawater intrusion. (4) Seawater intrusion can lead to deterioration of water quality in coastal areas and a reduction in irrigation water sources. Imbalance of soil ecosystem and degradation of cultivated land resources; It has affected industrial and agricultural production. Reduce the health level of the population and affect social stability; It will eventually lead to the deterioration of the natural ecological environment. (5) The main measures to prevent seawater intrusion in coastal areas include: rationally exploiting underground freshwater resources to increase revenue and reduce expenditure; Carry out artificial reinjection to divert fresh water and reduce salt water. Set up water barriers, build underground water-retaining DAMS, etc. to block water flow; Adjust the industrial structure and improve the ecological environment; Establish groundwater monitoring systems in coastal areas, etc.

Seawater intrusion is the result of the combined influence of two major factors: natural and human social and economic activities in a specific area. At present, many coastal areas around the world are suffering from the threat of seawater intrusion. In China, at least one city or region has experienced seawater intrusion of varying degrees to 11 degrees. Take Dalian as an example. There are several sections of the coastline where seawater intrusion occurred, with a cumulative intrusion area of 12,230 square kilometers. Due to the pollution of seawater, the chloride ion content in groundwater is as high as ~, with the maximum being 300 1,000 mg/L to 7,000 mg/L[1], which has led to the deterioration of water quality in most areas, land compaction and salinization, bringing multiple adverse effects and huge hazards to the social and economic development of this area. Seawater intrusion has become a serious environmental hydrogeological problem. Therefore, conducting research on seawater intrusion, formulating reasonable groundwater development, utilization and management plans for coastal and island areas, and preventing and mitigating the hazards caused by seawater intrusion have significant theoretical significance and scientific value.

As early as 1964, the phenomenon of seawater intrusion caused by excessive groundwater extraction occurred in Dalian City, China. At present, more than ten coastal cities have experienced varying degrees of seawater intrusion. The intrusion of seawater not only deteriorates the ecological environment of coastal areas, but also hinders the economic development of coastal regions. After experiencing rapid growth in the 1980s, the area of seawater intrusion in China's coastal regions has shown a slow growth trend in recent years. Even in some areas, the area of seawater intrusion no longer increases. In order to gain a deeper understanding of the mechanism of seawater intrusion, since the late 1970s, relevant scholars have successively carried out a series of studies, such as understanding the geochemical effects of seawater intrusion through water-rock cation exchange experiments, and establishing a three-dimensional finite element variable density transition zone numerical model to simulate and predict the process and development trend of seawater intrusion. To prevent and control seawater intrusion, in addition to controlling the amount and layout of groundwater extraction, coastal areas have also adopted corresponding prevention and control measures based on local conditions, including artificial replenishment, underground anti-seepage curtains, water-saving irrigation, and long-distance water diversion and other engineering measures.

The coastal areas are densely populated and have relatively developed industrial and agricultural production. However, the amount of freshwater resources is relatively limited. Therefore, the rational utilization and management of water resources are of special importance. In the 1960s, in China, there were only a few areas such as Dalian where seawater directly invaded freshwater aquifers. Since the 1970s, with the increase in groundwater extraction, sporadic seawater intrusions have occurred in various places one after another. In the 1980s, the scope of the invasion gradually expanded and the situation became increasingly serious. Among them, the more severe areas include Dalian, Qinhuangdao, Laizhou, Longkou and Fushan. Another similar situation is that salt water from Marine sediments formed in geological history on land crosses the original saltwater and freshwater interface position and invades the freshwater aquifer. To distinguish it from the former, people are accustomed to calling it 'saltwater invasion'. The intrusion of seawater and brackish water has brought serious harm to local industrial and agricultural production as well as people's lives. Studying the intrusion of seawater (brackish water) has become an urgent matter in our country. This article intends to take our work along the coast of Laizhou Bay during the 'Seventh Five-Year Plan' period as the basis and refer to the data from other regions to offer some immature views on the issue of seawater intrusion.

In many areas along the coasts of the Bohai Sea and the Yellow Sea in our country,  due to excessive groundwater exploitation in recent years,  has witnessed varying degrees of intensified seawater intrusion , with Shandong Province being the most prominent. Youdaoplaceholder3 incidents have also occurred in Hebei, Liaoning, Jiangsu, Tianjin, Shanghai and other provinces and cities. Youdaoplaceholder4 has seriously restricted the economic development of the coastal open areas. Taking the chloride ion concentration in well water greater than 0.3g/l as the standard for seawater intrusion,  by the end of 1995, the area of seawater intrusion in the Laizhou Bay area of Shandong Province had reached 974.6 km2. According to incomplete statistics, there are already 400,000 people in the  area facing water shortage , over 8,000 agricultural Wells have been scrapped , more than 600,000 mu of farmland have lost their irrigation capacity , the annual grain output has decreased by 300 million kilograms , and the annual industrial output value has lost 400 million yuan. If left unchecked,  within the next twenty years,  the area of seawater intrusion will reach 2,400 square kilometers , accounting for a quarter of the entire Laizhou Bay area. Therefore,  prevention and control of seawater intrusion has become an urgent matter.

'a. The transition zone model considering the influence of density change is the trend for the development of future numerical models of seawater intrusion. In recent years,  considering the chemical reaction effect during the mixing process of salt and fresh water and the influence of adsorption (desorption) dynamics on the surface of aquifer media on salt transport  some scholars have made active attempts in the coupling of hydrodynamics and chemical dynamics [13]. 'b. The Euler-Lagrange mixed solution is increasingly becoming the mainstream method for solving seawater intrusion water quality models , but further work is needed to promote the method. Recently, Oude Essink (2002) applied the MOCDENS3D program in the study of seawater intrusion in a coastal aquifer in the Netherlands, which focused on the influence of changes in groundwater density. 'c It is advisable to comprehensively evaluate and determine the degree and scope of seawater intrusion by using multiple indicators and methods. It should be pointed out that in addition to monitoring the water chemical composition changes of groundwater , the monitoring of  seawater intrusion monitoring should also attach importance to the monitoring of the properties of the surface soil. In addition,  given the unique advantages of boron isotopes in seawater intrusion monitoring,  it is recommended to consider the application of boron isotope methods in areas with complex conditions. Research on the water-rock (soil) reaction during the process of seawater intrusion should be strengthened. 'd The coastline should not be directly used as the coastal boundary of the aquifer system. On the gentle coast , the tidal effect information of groundwater can be utilized to determine the coastal boundary of the aquifer. 'e. The selection of hydrodynamic parameters in the model directly affects the accuracy of the model prediction. Research shows that the spatial variability of the water-bearing medium  leads to the scale effect nature of the aquifer parameters, while most models lack discussion on the selected parameters or have insufficient evidence. The author believes that the traditional hydrodynamic dispersion theory based on typical unit bodies of  cannot be used to describe the solute transport problem in complex aquifers on a large scale in the field. In recent years,  stochastic theory has become an important tool for studying the spatial variation characteristics and solute transport laws of water-bearing media on a macroscopic scale . However, most current research is still based on some assumptions (such as the second-order stability of the permeability coefficient InK, stable seepage field, etc.) . Therefore, more work needs to be done for the development of the stochastic theory of solute transport in groundwater to practical application . Therefore,  suggests strengthening the research on the solute transport theory in complex aquifers on a large scale in the field  attempts to describe the scale effect properties of aquifer parameters from a mechanistic perspective  to provide a basis for model establishment. 'f. The imbalance between supply and demand of water resources is the essential cause of seawater intrusion  the key to preventing seawater intrusion lies in 'prevention'. For areas where seawater intrusion has already occurred,  comprehensive management measures should be taken in accordance with local conditions  to reduce the damage caused by seawater intrusion  and prevent the aggravation of seawater intrusion.

Seawater intrusion is a very common and widely distributed pollution problem in coastal aquifers around the world. The best way to understand, predict, prevent and manage aquifers is to conduct monitoring and numerical simulation by computer. This paper selects the coastal plain on the east coast of Laizhou Bay in China as the research area. Through the research, the following important conclusions are obtained: (1) Sandy coastal landforms and quaternary sediments are widely distributed on the east coast of Laizhou Bay. Influenced by two Marine invasions in geological history, the continental aquifer formed by alluvial deposits extends far to the seabed. This coastal aquifer structure and coastal environment provide a very convenient passage for saltwater to invade freshwater areas. (2) The per capita water resources in Laizhou City are basically the same as those in Shandong Province, and the underground freshwater resources are relatively small. The analysis of the balance between water supply and demand shows that even after the completion of the South-to-North Water Diversion Project, the current water supply is still difficult to meet the demand. The shortage of water resources themselves and the over-exploitation of groundwater are the key factors that induce seawater intrusion. Laizhou City has been conducting systematic monitoring of groundwater levels and water quality since the late 1970s. Years of monitoring have shown that seawater intrusion has gone through three stages: initial development, accelerated development and retreat. The recent retreat of the brackish water interface from land to sea is mainly not due to the increase in precipitation or the reduction in underground freshwater extraction, but rather the extraction of brackish water from the nearshore seabed aquifer. (4) The seabed groundwater on the eastern shore of Laizhou Bay is distributed in the Quaternary sediments beneath the seabed. This type of salt water is stored in semi-confined aquifers and has a strong hydraulic connection with the fresh water stored in continental aquifers, and is replenished by fresh water. Now a large amount of salt water is being extracted. Studies have shown that characteristic ion values such as PH value, density, and sodium adsorption ratio (SAR) follow certain distribution patterns from land to sea. For instance, high-value nitrates in terrestrial aquifers are also well reflected in seabed groundwater. From land to sea, the water chemical types gradually transformed from Cl-Ca.Na.Mg type to Cl-NA.MG type and Cl-Na type. On the other hand, the ionic strength (IS) and the saturation index (SI) of mineral phases also follow certain distribution patterns, constituting the general hydrogeochemical evolution model of the coastal zone. The application of hydrochemical methods can relatively easily determine seawater intrusion. (5) In addition to hydrochemical methods, geophysical exploration techniques, including vertical resistivity sounding, transient electromagnetic sounding and excited polarization methods, can also be used to monitor the development of seawater intrusion. Meanwhile, geophysical logging techniques including Gamma ray and induced conductivity logging are also used for the detection of typical profiles. Through the interpretation of the detection curve, a series of monitoring indicators can be generated to determine the dynamic changes of the brackish water interface. Based on a large amount of field test data, this paper has obtained two types of indicators for seawater intrusion monitoring. One is the hydrogeochemical indicator composed of the ratio of characteristic chemical ions, and the other is the geophysical indicator composed of resistivity and Gamma value. The article also presents the corresponding relationship between the two types of indicators and introduces some matters that should be noted in the monitoring work. (6) Numerical simulation is the most important work in this paper. Firstly, a small-scale pumping test was conducted to verify the hydrogeological parameters, and it was found that the effect was quite good. Therefore, a large amount of similar pumping test data in this area can be used as the parameters required for computer simulation.

The evaluation results show that the seawater intrusion in the study area is distributed in strips, from north to south, they are the severely invaded area, the invaded area and the non-invaded area in sequence. Among them, the severely invaded areas are mainly distributed in Dajiawa, Yangzi and Xiaying regions, with an invaded area of 2,073 square kilometers, accounting for 43.7% of the total area. The invasion area is located in the area of Taitou and Changyi. The invasion area is 547 square kilometers, accounting for 11.5% of the total area, and the maximum invasion distance can reach 30 kilometers. The non-invasive areas are mainly distributed in the urban areas of Shouguang and Weifang as well as south of the urban area of Changyi, accounting for 44.8% of the total area. A seawater intrusion risk evaluation index system with 5 factors and 27 variables was established. The risk of seawater intrusion in the coastal area of Weifang was evaluated based on the information volume model. The risk evaluation results divided the northern coastal area of Weifang into 4 levels: high-risk area, relatively high-risk area, low-risk area and no-risk area. Through calculation, the area of the high-risk zone is 2,138 square kilometers, accounting for 45.4% of the total evaluated area. The area of the high-risk zone is 521 square kilometers, accounting for 11.0% of the total evaluated area. The area of the low-risk zone is 479 square kilometers, accounting for 10.0% of the total evaluated area. The area of the no-hazard zone is 1,583 square kilometers, accounting for 33.6% of the total evaluated area. It can be seen that approximately two-thirds of the entire Weifang coastal area is located in the risk zone of seawater intrusion disasters. The sequential indication simulation method was adopted to carry out the early warning evaluation of seawater intrusion in Laizhou Bay area. According to the probability of disaster occurrence and the risk assessment level, the early warning evaluation levels of seawater intrusion were divided into four grades, and the early warning evaluation of seawater intrusion in Laizhou Bay area in 2013 was conducted. The early warning results show that the seawater intrusion early warning areas in the entire study area are distributed in a band-like pattern, and the early warning levels gradually decrease from north to south. Based on the results of the early warning evaluation and the characteristics of the causes of seawater intrusion, targeted countermeasures for the prevention and control of seawater intrusion are comprehensively proposed from three aspects: engineering technology, ecological restoration and administrative management.

Since the late 1970s, the southern shore area of Laizhou Bay has been in a period of rapid economic development and a sharp increase in population pressure. The disturbance caused by human activities to the environment has become particularly prominent. The Laizhou Bay area, with its unique geographical location, geological environment evolution background and sensitivity to climate change, has become the region in China that is most severely affected by natural disasters caused by human activities and natural factors It is a typical area invaded by sea (salt) water from our country to the world, which has formed a serious obstacle to the sustainable development of the economy, society and environment. Thanks to the efforts of scientists, remarkable achievements have been made in the research on the geological and geomorphic background of seawater intrusion in Laizhou Bay, the zoning, types and prevention of seawater intrusion, and the basic understanding of Laizhou has been clarified The development process of seawater intrusion along the coast of Laizhou Bay. It is believed that the channels, types and ranges of seawater intrusion in each area along the coast of Laizhou Bay are closely related to the background of Quaternary environmental evolution in each area. The pre-test of seawater intrusion also achieved a satisfactory progress. From the perspective of governance, two demonstration zones for seawater intrusion control have been established in the Wang River Basin of Laizhou and Shouguang, with remarkable results [1-14]. Since the 90th year of the last century, scientists' research has mostly focused on the treatment methods and effectiveness evaluation of Marine water intrusion, paying less attention to the latest dynamics. Most of the original causes are described in a definite way, while there are relatively few explorations in a definite quantity. Based on the comprehensive research and monitoring results of various institutions, this paper will present the latest development trends of seawater intrusion on the southern shore of Laizhou Bay and quantitatively explore the development of seawater intrusion and groundwater by using neural network technology The relationship between over-recovery and the reduction in water volume.

The coastal zone is located at the junction of the continent and the ocean, and the estuaries of major rivers are the key coastal zones. The coastal zone has a strong water exchange capacity and relatively abundant underground water resources. However, there is an unreasonable temporal distribution and uneven spatial distribution of water resource runoff. For instance, coastal cities such as Dalian, Qinhuangdao, Tianjin, Qingdao, Wenzhou, Xiamen and Beihai have successively witnessed resource-based water shortage and water quality-based water shortage [1,2], and the carrying capacity of resources and the environment has significantly declined. The fragile ecological environment of the coastal zone has become an important limiting factor for the economic and social development of key coastal zones in China [3]. The ecological environment problems in the coastal zone are closely related to groundwater, especially having a direct connection with the unreasonable exploitation of groundwater resources. The intensification of human engineering activities has led to significant changes in groundwater levels, water quality or energy. The dynamic balance of the water cycle involved in groundwater, rock (soil) hydraulics and groundwater flow fields has all been disrupted, and numerous environmental geological problems will arise in the coastal zone. Such as the continuous decline of regional groundwater levels, intrusion of seawater (brackish water), soil salinization, ground subsidence, and groundwater quality pollution, etc. Among them, the intrusion of seawater (brackish) is one of the important problems caused by the linkage of coastal water systems. When the balance between fresh water in the aquifer on land and seawater (brackish water) in the ocean is disrupted, seawater (or brackish water with hydraulic connection to seawater) invades along the aquifer towards the land, thus forming the phenomenon of seawater (brackish water) intrusion, which causes severe pollution to the underground fresh water on land.

Modern seawater intrusion is no longer merely a theoretical issue of underground hydrodynamics, but a highly interdisciplinary and comprehensive subject. Therefore, supplementing, improving and developing the original theoretical system has become a key issue in the study of Marine intrusion. On one hand, it is necessary to intensify the research on the weak links in the original rational reasoning methods, such as the extraction methods of various environmental parameters, the research and development of various models, and the reasonable adjustment of the human-land relationship in the intrusion area of seawater. On the other hand, It is necessary to integrate the research on Marine intrusion with resource conservation and environmental improvement in accordance with the original principles of the geological system. To ultimately achieve a clear and obvious comprehensive benefit in terms of economy, society and the environment.

The environmental problems caused by seawater intrusion have an adverse impact on the economic construction and social development of the area on the east bank of the Pearl River Estuary. Accurately defining the scope of seawater intrusion  and analyzing the dynamic development of seawater intrusion under different mining conditions  can provide a scientific basis for preventing and reducing seawater intrusion. Based on the systematic analysis of the geological and hydrogeological conditions in the study area, a three-dimensional solute model of seawater intrusion in the eastern bank of the pearl River estuary was established through the correct generalization of hydrogeological conditions . The established mathematical model was solved using FEFLOW  The dynamic observation data of underground water level and concentration  were used to identify and verify the model. The identified models were used to simulate the trend of seawater intrusion in the  study area under different mining conditions. It is indicated that reducing the amount of underground water exploitation  the area of seawater intrusion can gradually decrease  the intrusion can be effectively controlled. In addition,  under the condition of reducing the extraction volume,  compared with the water-bearing layer of the bedrock fissure, the volume shrinkage of the seawater intrusion surface in the quaternary water-bearing layer is slower . This is because, except for the influence of the underground water extraction volume,  the water ratio of the quaternary loose rock fissure in the coastal area is lower than that of the bedrock Fissured water is more susceptible to the influence of seawater tides

Beihai City is located on the southern coast of Guangxi. Generally, it has a peninsular terrain that protrudes westward, with a flat and open ground. The coastal plain is distributed with the middle and lower Holocene of the Fourth system and the loose sediment of the upper Third system. From top to bottom, there are submerged water-bearing layers and confined water-bearing layers. There exists a close hydrodynamic connection between the two through the weakly permeable water layer between them. The confined aquifer is thick, has good permeability and the groundwater level is shallowly buried, making it the exploitation layer of groundwater in Beihai City. Since 2010, especially since the beginning of the human age, the urban construction of Beihai City has developed rapidly, the population has grown rapidly, and the amount of groundwater extraction has increased year by year. Unreasonable exploitation of underground water has led to the occurrence of Marine and water intrusion in the northern coastal zone. What characteristics the chemical composition of groundwater in the coastal aquifer of Beihai City has and what changes have occurred in its water quality have always been issues of concern to all sectors of local society. This article is based on the water quality monitoring conducted in Beihai City since the late 1980s. Water samples are taken twice a year during the dry season and the wet season for comprehensive water quality analysis data, as well as some monitoring data from drilling. Conduct a preliminary analysis of the water-related chemical characteristics of underground water.

In order to identify the main factors of groundwater salinization and the mechanism of seawater intrusion in the Yang-Daihe Coastal Plain, different types of water samples (river water, shallow fresh water, brackish water, rainwater, seawater and deep water) were collected in this paper. By analyzing the water chemical characteristics, ion ratios, Gibbs diagrams, tritium and oxygen isotope compositions, and mineral phase equilibrium states of different water bodies in this area, the Yang-Daihe coastal Plain was studied The hydrogeochemical processes of different water bodies in the seawater intrusion area of the Marine plain. The results show that the evolution of river water and shallow freshwater in the Yang-Daihe Coastal Plain is mainly influenced by rock and soil weathering and leaching, and is also affected by evaporation and concentration during this process. The formation of brackish water is mainly controlled by the mixing of shallow fresh water and seawater and the evaporation and concentration of shallow fresh water. The local groundwater salinization may have been replenished by the overflow of deep underground hot water (water sample Q06), and some deep groundwater has also been mixed with seawater (water sample Q35). Based on the analysis of the mixing ratio of seawater and the mineral saturation index SI, it was revealed that the maximum mixing ratio of seawater during the mixing process of shallow fresh water and seawater was 4.35%, during which the exchange of Ca and Na ions mainly occurred. In different water bodies, calcite and dolomite are basically in a saturated state, while gypsum, anhydrous gypsum and rock salt have not reached a saturated state.

Seawater intrusion has become a major disaster phenomenon in coastal areas. It is a hydrodynamic process in which seawater or brackish water seeps and diffuses into inland freshwater bodies along underground pore media due to various natural factors (such as drought or rising sea levels) and human activities (mainly excessive exploitation of underground freshwater). It is mainly manifested in the increasing shortage of freshwater resources and the gradual deterioration of the ecological environment. The occurrence mechanism of seawater intrusion determines the complexity, concealment and variability of the development of such disasters, and these characteristics in turn determine the arduousness of the prevention and control of seawater intrusion. The damage and losses caused by seawater intrusion are huge and have become an important factor restricting the social and economic development of coastal areas, which has drawn full attention from coastal countries around the world. The ultimate goal of seawater intrusion research is to achieve the control of seawater intrusion disasters. At present, although it is impossible for humans to fundamentally eliminate such disasters, certain measures can be taken to mitigate them. At the very least, this can prevent the further aggravation of seawater intrusion caused by human activities. In recent years, theoretical research and practical application on suppressing or controlling seawater intrusion have received full attention from many countries. Many countries and regions have carried out theoretical research and practical application experiments on seawater intrusion control, achieving remarkable social, ecological and economic benefits. Theoretical analysis and practical application of the mechanism of seawater intrusion under various natural and human influencing factors should be the first step to successfully control seawater intrusion disasters. A large number of research results at home and abroad have made special elaborations on this. This article merely discusses the application technology of comprehensive prevention and control of seawater intrusion in light of the actual situation of seawater intrusion in China.

There are many geological factors influencing seawater intrusion, mainly including four types: coastal boundary slope, aquifer medium stratification, hydraulic conductivity coefficient and dispersion. Coastal boundaries are mainly vertical coastal boundaries and inclined coastal boundaries, and boundary conditions directly affect the circulation pattern of seawater in the boundary area. Aquifers can be classified into homogeneous aquifers and layered heterogeneous aquifers. The structure of aquifers controls the migration paths of water and salt in the brackish water transition zone. Meanwhile, the differences in hydraulic conductivity and dispersion directly affect the migration and distribution of the brackish water contact zone during the process of seawater intrusion. At the same time, the hydrodynamic conditions are classified into two categories: sea level changes and groundwater level changes. Both directly alter the hydraulic gradient between salt and fresh water, disrupt the original dynamic balance, and drive the development of seawater intrusion. Sea level changes can be classified into two types: sea level rise and periodic fluctuations of sea level. Both establish a new hydraulic gradient balance between salt and fresh water at different time scales, controlling the occurrence of seawater intrusion.

The water source area of Dalian Daweijia is located in a typical coastal karst area in northern China. Over the past 30 years, the unreasonable exploitation of underground fresh water has led to the groundwater level dropping funnel, which has triggered severe seawater intrusion. Taking the Daweijia water source area as the research object, through extensive hydrogeological investigations and water chemistry and isotope sampling, testing and analysis, the hydrodynamic conditions for the formation of seawater intrusion were explored. By analyzing the main water chemistry and multiple isotope (δ2H-δ18O,δ34S,δ13C) composition characteristics of groundwater in the coastal karst aquifer, the main occurrences during the seawater intrusion process were identified Hydrogeochemical processes were carried out and quantitatively simulated, thereby clarifying the mechanism of seawater intrusion in karst aquifers. The research results show that the main channels of seawater intrusion in Dalian Daweijia are the water-conducting faults, karst fissures and Quaternary loose strata existing in the Daweijia area. The composition analysis of δ2H-δ18O isotopes indicates that the groundwater in the study area mainly comes from the recharge of atmospheric precipitation. Combined with the concentration distribution of Cl-, it is believed that in addition to the increase of salt in groundwater after the intrusion of seawater into the freshwater aquifer, the evaporation of shallow groundwater also plays an important role in the accumulation of salt in groundwater. Based on the isotopic characteristics of δ34SSO4,δ13CHCO3, etc. in different water bodies, combined with the analysis of water chemical components (such as SO2-4,Cl-), it is believed that the brackish water and salt water in the study area are not simply a mixture of groundwater, fresh water and seawater. Reverse hydrogeochemical simulations have revealed that the main hydrogeochemical reactions controlling the hydrochemical evolution in coastal karst aquifers include the dissolution of calcite, montmorillonite and gypsum, the precipitation of illite, and the Ca-Na ion exchange, accompanied by the release of CO2.
